metadata.migrationNotes: sc-82 — Variant V1 of the swarm-bench harness (Epic 38: Self-Organizing Agents). Apples-to-apples comparison floor for V2 (sc-40 hand-authored Sequential subflow) and V3 (sc-43 Swarm node). One workflow `swarm-bench-baseline` v1 with two nodes: a Start that seeds workflow.mission from input via inputScript, and a single Agent that produces the answer and submits on a terminal port `Answered` (unwired so the agent's output becomes the workflow's terminal artifact). Authored at the library default (openai/gpt-5.4) to match V2; the bench harness in sc-41 pins all variants to one model — flip by editing the two agents (`swarm-bench-baseline-init`, `swarm-bench-baseline-agent`) and bumping versions before each run. The baseline-agent prompt is intentionally generalist — no role talk, no abstention protocol, no panel context — so any V2/V3 lift over V1 reflects emergent multi-perspective contribution rather than prompt sophistication.

inputs.description: The mission text the agent should produce an answer for. Use the same input text as the V2 / V3 variants in the bench so the comparison is apples-to-apples.

config.name: Swarm Bench Baseline Init
config.systemPrompt:
You are the entry-point agent for the swarm-bench baseline workflow. The mission has already been seeded into workflow state by an input script.

Your only job: write the single word `Initialized.` as your message content, then submit on port `Continue`.
config.promptTemplate: Mission has been seeded into the workflow bag as `workflow.mission`. Acknowledge and continue.

outputs.description: Hands off to the answering agent. The mission is already in the workflow bag; this agent does no setup of its own.
Hands off to the answering agent.

config.name: Swarm Bench Baseline Agent
config.systemPrompt:
You are an LLM tasked with producing the best single-shot answer to a mission. There is no panel and no protocol — just you and the mission. Aim for a complete, well-structured answer in the right size for what's asked.

Submit on port `Answered`. Write your full answer as the assistant message content before calling submit.

outputs.description: The agent's answer to the mission. Unwired in the workflow — the agent's message body is the workflow's terminal artifact.
Single-shot answer to the mission. Terminal artifact of the V1 baseline workflow.
